summary: AUDIT worker sweep — model/effort/rep parameterized; per-rep phase groups auto-create from the ACTUAL reps run (do NOT hardcode rep phases here, or the /workflows display implies more reps than execute).
logs: full-matrix mode: 1 reps × 1 models × 7 workers = 7 cells

glossary.location: CLAUDE.md § Glossary – first paragraph (Apps & plugins cross-repo vocabulary sentence)
glossary.claim: The cross-repo vocabulary (TP, TPP/TPS, NINA, TS/TSP, IS/ISP/ISS, XFM, TSM, AL) 'is defined once in the parent ../CLAUDE.md, which auto-loads in every astronomy subproject session.'
glossary.finding: E:\Projects\AI\CLAUDE.md does not exist. The file E:\Projects\AI\ contains only two entries: Skills/ and TargetPlanner/. Claude Code cannot auto-load a parent CLAUDE.md that has never been created.
glossary.evidence: Bash ls E:/Projects/AI/ returned only 'Skills' and 'TargetPlanner'; direct existence check confirmed NOT FOUND.

glossary.claim: ChartEvaluation is a 'single-field record… Carries just BrightnessInputsChanged; consumed only by the coordinator's post-apply hook to gate PushSkyKSInputs(ctx).'
glossary.finding: State/ChartEvaluation.cs defines three fields: required bool BrightnessInputsChanged, int EnsureWork, and int RenderWork. EnsureWork and RenderWork were added to drive the coordinator's two-phase progress bar (pre-sizing EnsureAsync work + Render work) and are read by ChartCoordinator.RunPipelineAsync. The record is no longer single-field.
glossary.evidence: E:\Projects\AI\TargetPlanner\TargetPlanner\State\ChartEvaluation.cs:25-40 (BrightnessInputsChanged, EnsureWork, RenderWork fields); ChartCoordinator.cs:200-213 (coordinator reads EnsureWork and RenderWork to size the progress bar).

glossary.claim: ActiveFilter is the 'Filters.Filter record (now positional record carrying Name + Lorentzian + CenterNm + BandwidthNm)'.
glossary.finding: Filters/Filter.cs defines a 9-parameter positional record: Name, SeparationDeg, WidthDays, RelaxEnabled, RelaxMinAltDeg, RelaxMaxAltDeg, RelaxScale, CenterNm, BandwidthNm. No property named 'Lorentzian' exists. The Relax extension fields (RelaxEnabled, RelaxMinAltDeg, RelaxMaxAltDeg, RelaxScale) are entirely absent from the description; what the doc calls 'Lorentzian' apparently predates the Relax split but the label now matches no actual symbol.
glossary.evidence: E:\Projects\AI\TargetPlanner\TargetPlanner\Filters\Filter.cs:28-37 (full positional constructor); PlanningPolicy.cs:29 ('Lorentzian + Relax fields, surfaced via MoonProfile') confirms the Relax group is separate.

glossary.location: CLAUDE.md § Glossary – ChartCoordinator bullet (pipeline step listing)
glossary.claim: Pipeline step listed as '→ mRenderActiveArea(ctx)' — one argument.
glossary.finding: ChartCoordinator.mRenderActiveArea is declared as Action<ChartContext, IProgress<(int Done, int Total)>> (two-arg delegate) and is called as mRenderActiveArea(ctx, renderProgress) at line 220. The single-arg pseudocode is factually wrong after the progress-bar addition.

conventions.claim: Current Log.Diag categories include `Sky` — listed as one of six in: `Coord`, `Cache`, `Day`, `Sky`, `UI`, `Overlay`.
conventions.finding: No `Log.Diag("Sky", ...)` call exists anywhere in the TP source. `AltitudeSubChart_Sky.cs` has no diag logging whatsoever. The `Sky` category is listed but unused.
conventions.evidence: Grep `Log.Diag("Sky"` across all TP .cs files returns zero matches. All other five categories have active callers: `Coord` in State/ChartCoordinator.cs:196, `Cache` in Caches/ChartCacheStore.cs:192, `Day` in Charts/AltitudeSubChart_Day.cs:348, `UI` in Forms/Presenters/*.cs, `Overlay` in Charts/AltitudeSubChart_Day.cs:210.

conventions.claim: Pattern C is described as filling 'any null/empty top-level field from the seed' as an additive-schema migration self-heal.
conventions.finding: The code only fills three specific fields: `NinaTargetsRoot`, `ImageLibraryRoot`, and `NamedLocations`. `LastSelectedLocationName` is a fourth top-level string field in `AppSettings` and is NOT Pattern-C filled. The word 'any' overstates the scope and would mislead a developer adding a new `AppSettings` field who might assume automatic fill is provided.
conventions.evidence: SettingsStore.cs:62-68 contains exactly three `if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(...))` / null-check fills for `NinaTargetsRoot`, `ImageLibraryRoot`, and `NamedLocations`. AppSettings.cs:26 declares `LastSelectedLocationName` as a top-level string property with no corresponding Pattern-C fill logic.

architecture.location: CLAUDE.md §Architecture — Cache state bullet (line 81): "single-writer four-axis cache: `(Location, Target)` yearDays"
architecture.claim: The yearDays cache axis is keyed by `(Location, Target)` — a compound key including Location.
architecture.finding: The actual CacheAxis generic parameter is `Target` only (`CacheAxis<Target, TargetCacheEntry>`). Per-location isolation is achieved by `SetLocationAsync` draining all four axes on a location change, not by including Location in the key. The other three axes in the same sentence correctly match their actual tuple key types (`(Target, HdmKey)`, `(Target, NightDate)`, `NightDate`), so the notation inconsistency here is misleading for anyone reading the cache structure.
architecture.evidence: Caches/ChartCacheStore.cs:65 — `private readonly CacheAxis<Target, TargetCacheEntry> mYearDaysAxis;`. Code comment at line 58 says "yearDays is per-target".

architecture.claim: RenderArea's pipeline is: Render → ShowOnlyAltitudeChart → Refresh (three steps).
architecture.finding: The actual `RenderArea` method has a fourth step between `ShowOnlyAltitudeChart` and `sc.Control.Refresh()`: it calls `ResizeAltitudeChartArea(sc.IdealHeight)` to grow the Panel/GroupBox/Form to match the sub-chart's current ideal height. This step is omitted from the doc's pipeline description. Additionally, the first step in the real code is `sc.Render(ctx, mCache, progress)` (3 args), not the 2-arg form shown.

architecture.location: CLAUDE.md §Architecture — Threading paragraph (line 85): "cache pre-population is 2–4 sec for 44 targets (yearDays)"
architecture.claim: Cache pre-population (yearDays) for 44 targets takes 2–4 seconds.
architecture.finding: Both the IChartCacheStore XML doc and the ChartCacheStore class summary consistently state "~1-2 sec for 44 targets post-CS-removal". The doc's 2–4 sec figure is 2× the code's own estimate. Because the doc uses this figure as a performance ceiling for future cache work ("Design future cache work against this ceiling"), the discrepancy matters.
architecture.evidence: Caches/ChartCacheStore.cs:34 — "Compute is short (~1-2 sec for 44 targets post-CS-removal)". Caches/IChartCacheStore.cs:36 — "Compute is short (~1-2 sec for 44 targets)".

deps-build.location: CLAUDE.md line 74 — '## External dependencies that are easy to miss'
deps-build.claim: NuGet packages on the WinExe project include `Velopack 0.0.1298`
deps-build.finding: Velopack version is stale. The csproj pins `0.0.1589-ga2c5a97`, not `0.0.1298`.

deps-build.location: CLAUDE.md line 65 — build bullets under '## Tool preference for symbol queries'
deps-build.finding: The solution has only two configurations: `Debug|x64` and `Release|x64`. The two AnyCPU configurations do not exist in the sln.
deps-build.evidence: TargetPlanner.sln lines 17-22 (SolutionConfigurationPlatforms block): only `Debug|x64 = Debug|x64` and `Release|x64 = Release|x64` are listed.

deps-build.location: CLAUDE.md line 66 — build bullets under '## Tool preference for symbol queries'
deps-build.claim: The two sibling-library ProjectReferences consumed by the csproj are `..\..\Library\Astronomy.Core\Astronomy.Core.csproj` and `..\..\Library\Astronomy.NINA\Astronomy.NINA.csproj`
deps-build.finding: The csproj has a third ProjectReference to `Astronomy.Diagnostics` (added 2026-06-11) that is absent from this list. Astronomy.Diagnostics is referenced by the csproj but is NOT listed as a sln-visible project, making it invisible to the build bullets' solution inventory.
deps-build.evidence: TargetPlanner/TargetPlanner.csproj line 70: `<ProjectReference Include="..\..\Library\Astronomy.Diagnostics\Astronomy.Diagnostics.csproj" />`. The path of the Library-side project itself is unverifiable (sibling repo absent) per audit rules.

deps-build.location: CLAUDE.md lines 54–68 — '## Build / run' heading vs '## Tool preference for symbol queries'
deps-build.claim: The '## Build / run' section says 'csproj-level gotchas and test-project scoping rationale below are kept here' but contains no build bullets. The actual build content (solution project inventory, TargetFramework, configurations, build commands, test invocation) appears under the immediately-following '## Tool preference for symbol queries' heading.
deps-build.finding: '## Build / run' is an empty section whose promised content is stranded under '## Tool preference for symbol queries', producing a misleading heading and burying build gotchas under a symbol-query section.
deps-build.evidence: CLAUDE.md line 54: '## Build / run' header + lines 55–58 preamble with no bullets. CLAUDE.md lines 64–68: solution structure, TFM, configurations, build commands, and test invocation command live under '## Tool preference for symbol queries' (line 60).

core-contract.claim: Four sub-bullets (IReadOnlyList returns, DateTime.Kind UTC contract, signed-degree inputs, no static mutable state) detailing Core API guarantees placed in the always-loaded router.
core-contract.finding: Detailed Core API reference content is off-charter for the CLAUDE.md router (charter: always-loaded router + load-bearing gotchas, kept thin). DOMAIN.md's own charter names it as the home for "the astronomy/coordinate/observing rules… the why and the contract," and its Status section (lines 10–26) explicitly lists this section as pending graduation to DOMAIN.md: "When you next touch a domain convention, graduate its canonical statement here and leave a one-line pointer at the old location."
core-contract.evidence: DOMAIN.md:10-19 — Status block explicitly cross-references `CLAUDE.md §"Core consumer contract"` as un-graduated content pending migration to DOMAIN.md. ARCHITECTURE.md:3 also redirects to CLAUDE.md for this content, confirming neither reference doc absorbed it. The four sub-bullets enumerate Core method names (VisibilityWindows.For, QualitySamples.OverNight, MoonSeparation.IntervalsAboveDeg, TargetGeometry.*), return-type contracts, and Core-internal class names (TimeKindGuard) — reference depth beyond a gotcha pointer.

docmap.location: CLAUDE.md ## Documentation map — journal section, line 26-27
docmap.claim: docs/*.md + docs/design/*.md are 'substantial standalone records (YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md): reviews, decisions, designs'
docmap.finding: 4 of 6 files in docs/design/ do not carry the YYYY-MM-DD- date prefix: cache-contract.md, chart-fits-cache.md, personal-defaults-and-settings.md, test-project-plan.md. Only 2026-05-17-architecture-review.md and 2026-05-26-code-review-async-ui.md match the stated convention.
docmap.evidence: E:\Projects\AI\TargetPlanner\docs\design\ listing: cache-contract.md, chart-fits-cache.md, personal-defaults-and-settings.md, test-project-plan.md, 2026-05-17-architecture-review.md, 2026-05-26-code-review-async-ui.md

crossref.claim: The parent portfolio glossary is defined in `'..\.\CLAUDE.md'` (rendered as `../CLAUDE.md`), which "auto-loads in every astronomy subproject session".
crossref.finding: The file `E:\Projects\AI\CLAUDE.md` (one directory above the project root) does not exist. The link is dangling.

crossref.claim: Inline image `![TargetPlanner v1.0.0 ...](docs/screenshot.png)` references a screenshot at `docs/screenshot.png`.
crossref.finding: `E:\Projects\AI\TargetPlanner\docs\screenshot.png` does not exist. The image reference is dangling — the README banner is broken.
crossref.evidence: Bash `ls E:/Projects/AI/TargetPlanner/docs/screenshot.png` → SCREENSHOT_MISSING; `find docs/` output lists no `.png` file.

crossref.claim: Link `[docs/code-quality-audit.md](docs/code-quality-audit.md)` points to the per-item details of the 2026-05-19 six-tier audit.
crossref.finding: No file at that path. The actual file is `E:\Projects\AI\TargetPlanner\docs\2026-05-19-code-quality-audit.md` (date-prefixed, matching the journal naming convention).

crossref.claim: "User-facing UX in [README.md](README.md#sky-brightness-day-sky-sub-mode)" — links to the anchor `#sky-brightness-day-sky-sub-mode` in README.
crossref.finding: No such anchor exists in README.md. The relevant section heading is `## Sky brightness` (README.md:98), which produces the anchor `#sky-brightness`. The slug `#sky-brightness-day-sky-sub-mode` is a rename orphan.
crossref.evidence: README.md:98 `## Sky brightness`; no heading matching `sky-brightness-day-sky-sub-mode` found anywhere in README.md.
